REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS RICHARD CHRISTENSEN AND BDS COUTURE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE FACTS CONCLUSIVELY ESTABLISHED BY THE OCTOBER 23, 2014 DISCOVERY ORDER SHOW DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. PLAINTIFFS ADMIT THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS MOOT BECAUSE BDS HAS DECLARED BANKRUPTCY. PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT THE ESTABLISHED FACTS DO NOT SHOW THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF THE REDUCED RENT WHICH PLAINTIFF AGREED TO ACCEPT FROM DEFENDANT IS MERITLESS; FACT B OF THE OCTOBER 23, 2014 ORDER ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF ACCEPTED ALL PAYMENTS MADE BY DEFENDANTS AS PAYMENT IN FULL. SIMILARLY, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO PROPERTY TAX AND INSURANCE PAYMENTS IS ALSO MERITLESS; THESE PAYMENTS ARE DEFINED AS RENT IN THE LEASE AND THEREFORE COVERED BY THE ESTABLISHED FACTS. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT THE ESTABLISHED FACTS DO NOT SHOW IT RELEASED DEFENDANT CHRISTENSEN FROM HIS GUARANTY IS IRRELEVANT; IF NO MONEY IS OWED UNDER THE LEASE THERE IS NOTHING PLAINTIFF CAN COLLECT FROM CHRISTENSEN UNDER A GUARANTY THEORY. DEFENDANTS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT AN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendants Richard A. Christensen And Bds Couture, Inc. , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTs RICHARD CHRISTENSEN and BDS COUTURE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. The facts conclusively established by the October 23, 2014 discovery order show Defendants are entitled to summary judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiffs admit the first cause of action is moot because BDS has declared bankruptcy.

Plaintiff's contention that the established facts do not show the dollar amount of the reduced rent which Plaintiff agreed to accept from Defendant is meritless; Fact B of the October 23, 2014 Order establishes that Plaintiff accepted all payments made by Defendants as payment in full. Similarly, Plaintiff's claim that Plaintiff is entitled to property tax and insurance payments is also meritless; these payments are defined as rent in the lease and therefore covered by the established facts. Finally, Plaintiff's contention that the established facts do not show it released Defendant Christensen from his Guaranty is irrelevant; if no money is owed under the lease there is nothing Plaintiff can collect from Christensen under a Guaranty theory.

Defendants to prepare and submit an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
